title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
summary: A music producer created a track using Suno AI's generative music tools, challenging the perception that AI-generated music is inherently boring. The track "Semiramis' Dream" features infectious jungle beats and has garnered genuine appreciation despite the author's typical skepticism toward AI-generated music.
content:
1010Benja won’t apologize for using AI. | Image: 1010Benja / Instagram	

I would never go so far as to say there's no place for AI in music (I'm a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially the outputs of Suno. So I'm having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that I actually quite enjoy 1010Benja's "Semiramis' Dream."
Benja has been unapologetic about his use of generative AI on his latest EP, Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… The other three tracks can't quite hold a candle to what you find on 2024's Ten Total. But the opener "Semiramis' Dream" is infectious. It explodes out of the speakers with a jungle beat and Be …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 我讨厌我不讨厌这首用Suno制作的歌曲
zh.summary: 一位音乐制作人使用Suno AI的生成音乐工具创作了一首曲目,挑战了人工智能生成音乐本质上枯燥的认知。这首名为《塞米拉米斯之梦》的曲目具有感染力十足的丛林节拍,尽管作者通常对AI生成音乐持怀疑态度,但仍获得了真诚的赞赏。

title: The FBI reportedly won’t investigate ICE anymore
summary: Federal authorities have reportedly changed policy to prevent the FBI from investigating confrontations involving ICE agents, according to the New York Times, though the DHS and DOJ deny the claim. The reported shift follows recent high-profile civilian deaths at the hands of ICE agents and concerns about witness intimidation.
content:
ICE agents hide behind masks while skulking through the halls of immigration court | Image: Michael Nigro/Pacific Press/LightRocket via Getty Images	

According to the New York Times, federal agents have been told that the FBI will no longer be investigating confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ denied the change in policy to The Times. 
The reported change in guidance follows renewed scrutiny of violence by ICE agents who have killed two people in the last two weeks. The shootings in Maine and Texas were just the latest civilian deaths at the hands of ICE agents, who have attempted to intimidate witnesses following its recent spate of high-profile killings. 
The new guidance would end FBI investigations of assaults against DHS agents. The government has aggressively pursu …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 据报道FBI将不再调查ICE
zh.summary: 据《纽约时报》报道,联邦当局已改变政策,防止FBI调查涉及ICE特工的对峙事件,尽管国土安全部和司法部否认这一说法。这一据称的政策转变发生在ICE特工最近造成的几起引人注目的平民死亡事件以及对证人恐吓的担忧之后。

title: TechCrunch Mobility: The battle over robotaxi rules
summary: TechCrunch Mobility examines the regulatory challenges and policy debates surrounding autonomous taxi services as cities and states work to establish rules governing AI-powered transportation systems.
content: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

zh.title: TechCrunch Mobility:机器人出租车规则之争
zh.summary: TechCrunch Mobility审视了自主出租车服务周围的监管挑战和政策辩论,因为各城市和州正在努力建立管理人工智能驱动交通系统的规则。

title: Orchid is a delightfully retro and approachable hipster synth
summary: Telepathic Instruments' Orchid synthesizer revives the vintage appeal of electric chord organs, allowing users to play full chord progressions with simple button presses rather than complex fingerwork. The device targets both music enthusiasts and casual players seeking accessible music-making tools inspired by 1960s-era instruments.
content:
A modern tribute to electric chord organs which were basically giant harmonicas connected to a fan. | Photo: Terrence O’Brien / The Verge	

In 2017, I bought an old Magnus chord organ off Craigslist for $10. It's one of my favorite music gear purchases. Electric chord organs let you play full chords with just a press of a button, making them perfect for aspiring singer-songwriters or fooling around at home. But chord organs largely fell out of favor in the '80s, as digital keyboards like those from Casio grew in popularity.
The Telepathic Instruments Orchid attempts to recapture some of that simple parlor instrument magic. Just like those vintage electric chord organs, the Orchid allows you to play rich chord progressions without having to master a ton of theory or complex fing …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Orchid是一款令人愉悦的复古且易上手的时髦合成器
zh.summary: Telepathic Instruments公司的Orchid合成器复兴了电动和弦风琴的复古魅力,让用户只需按几个按钮就能演奏完整的和弦进行,无需复杂的指法。该设备针对音乐爱好者和寻求易用音乐制作工具的休闲用户,灵感来自1960年代的乐器。

title: Birdfy’s solar-powered smart feeder is down to one of its best prices
summary: Birdfy's AI-powered smart bird feeder with integrated camera and solar panel is available at a significant discount, falling to $149.99 from its $259.99 list price. The device features 1080p night vision, real-time motion alerts, and AI identification of over 6,000 bird species without requiring a subscription.
content:
It comes with a solar panel so you don’t need to worry about charging as much during the summer. | Image: Netvue	

Birdfy has kicked off a midyear sale, taking up to 40 percent off a range of its smart bird feeders. One of the best deals is on Netvue’s Birdfy Feeder AI-Powered Smart Bird Feeder with Camera, which has dropped to $149.99 at Birdfy and is its second-best price to date. The bundle, which includes a solar panel and lifetime AI bird identification at no extra cost, comes with a $259.99 list price, saving you $110.
Birdfy Feeder

Where to Buy:

 $259.99 $149.99 at Birdfy (solar-powered)

The feeder features a wide-angle 1080p camera with color night vision, allowing you to watch birds in clear detail day or night. It also sends real-time motion alerts to the Birdfy app, where you can watch the livefeed or short, recorded videos, as well as learn more about the birds that visit your feeder. The included AI can identify more than 6,000 bird species without requiring a subscription, and can also recognize squirrels and other animals, helping you tell what triggered an alert.
The IP65-rated feeder features a large 1.5-liter seed container, so you won’t have to refill it as often. Meanwhile, the included solar panel means you likely won’t have to manually recharge the battery, so long as it receives enough sunlight. You also get local and cloud video storage options, with the company offering three months of free cloud storage.

zh.title: Birdfy太阳能智能喂鸟器降价至历史最低价之一
zh.summary: Birdfy公司的AI驱动智能喂鸟器配备集成摄像头和太阳能面板,现在以重大折扣提供,从原价259.99美元降至149.99美元。该设备具有1080p夜视功能、实时运动警报和可识别超过6000种鸟类物种的AI识别功能,无需订阅。

title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
summary: Director Christopher Nolan has characterized artificial intelligence as an obvious "Trojan horse," suggesting concealed risks lie within its widespread adoption and integration into society.

zh.title: 《奥德赛》导演克里斯托弗·诺兰称人工智能是明显的"木马"
zh.summary: 导演克里斯托弗·诺兰将人工智能描述为一个明显的"木马",暗示在其广泛采用和整合到社会中隐含着隐藏的风险。

title: US Marshals arrest the Tate brothers in Miami
summary: Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested by US Marshals in Miami on new charges including rape, sex trafficking, and child exploitation crimes in the United Kingdom. British authorities are seeking their extradition to face the additional charges, which come on top of 21 charges already filed against them in 2025.
content:
Andrew and Tristan Tate talk to the media on March 23, 2025 in Romania. | Image: Andrei Pungovschi/Getty Images	

The manosphere influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested Saturday in Miami by US Marshals in relation to new rape and sex trafficking charges in England. According to the Associated Press, British authorities are seeking the brothers' extradition. 
The new charges facing Andrew Tate include seven counts of rape, three of sex trafficking, three counts of assault, and "19 charges relating to sexual images of children and extreme 'pornography,'" according to the New York Times. Tristan Tate faces two new charges of rape, one of sexual assault, and three of sex trafficking. This is in addition to the 21 charges already announced in 2025 …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 美国法警在迈阿密逮捕塔特兄弟
zh.summary: 安德鲁和特里斯坦·塔特在迈阿密被美国法警逮捕，面临包括强奸、贩运妇女和英国儿童剥削犯罪的新指控。英国当局正寻求对他们进行引渡，以面对这些额外指控，这是在2025年已对他们提出的21项指控之外。

title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
summary: Current AI, a nonprofit organization, is developing accessible artificial intelligence tools designed to represent and serve all cultures equitably across diverse devices and chat platforms. The organization has made significant progress in creating culturally inclusive AI systems.
content: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.

zh.title: 非营利组织Current AI正在竞速构建供所有人免费使用的人工智能万维网
zh.summary: Current AI是一个非营利组织，正在开发可访问的人工智能工具，旨在跨不同设备和聊天平台公平地代表和服务所有文化。该组织在创建文化包容性人工智能系统方面已取得重大进展。

title: The Clapper was a bad smart home gadget — and a viral sensation
summary: The Clapper was an unreliable voice-activated home control device that became a cultural phenomenon despite its spotty performance and potential to damage connected devices. The product's success was driven more by a memorable advertising campaign than by its actual functionality.
content:
Clap on. Clap off. Well, more like, Clap, pause for half a beat but no longer because otherwise it'll stop hearing you, clap again because you waited too long, clap louder and faster, that didn't work, clap two more times, and suddenly: on. The Clapper didn't always work - and even when it did, it might fry your gadgets in the process - but it managed to become a big hit anyway.
On this episode of Version History, we tell the story of The Clapper, from its beginnings as The Great American Turn-On to its debut alongside one of the great advertising campaigns of all time. The Verge's David Pierce and Victoria Song are joined by Allison Marsh, …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Clapper是一个不好的智能家居小工具——却成为了病毒式轰动
zh.summary: Clapper是一个不可靠的语音激活家庭控制设备，尽管其性能参差不齐且可能损害连接的设备，但它成为了文化现象。该产品的成功更多是由令人难忘的广告活动驱动的，而不是由其实际功能驱动的。

title: The future of physical games is not looking great
summary: Grand Theft Auto VI will not include a physical disc release, marking another major franchise's transition to digital-only distribution. This shift reflects the gaming industry's broader move away from physical media, eliminating retail experiences and physical game collections that defined earlier gaming culture.
content:
Grand Theft Auto VI release won’t offer a disc. | Image: Rockstar Games	

This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on video games and physical media, follow Jay Peters. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes on Sunday at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.
How it started
As a kid, I relished trips to Best Buy, GameStop, and the nearby mall so that I could browse video games. I loved sifting through games and chatting with my friends at the store about upcoming releases. On the lucky days I actually got a new game from the store, I treasured reading through every page of the manual on the drive home. Over the years, I built up a collecti …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 实体游戏的未来前景堪忧
zh.summary: 《侠盗猎车手VI》将不再发行物理光盘版本，标志着又一个大型游戏特许经营权向数字专属发行的转变。这一趋势反映了游戏产业更广泛地放弃实体媒体，消除了零售体验和早期游戏文化中定义的实体游戏收藏。

title: As mosquito ranges expand, better monitoring is key to preventing disease
summary: Expanding mosquito ranges driven by climate change are increasing the demand for comprehensive disease monitoring by public health agencies. While expensive and labor-intensive, such monitoring programs remain critical for early detection and prevention of disease outbreaks before they spread widely.
content: Monitoring is expensive and labor intensive. But it helps public health officials stop outbreaks.

zh.title: 蚊虫活动范围扩大，更好的监测对预防疾病至关重要
zh.summary: 气候变化驱动的蚊虫活动范围扩大，增加了公共卫生机构进行全面疾病监测的需求。尽管成本高昂且劳动密集，但此类监测计划对于早期发现和防止疾病爆发蔓延至关重要。

title: The grueling, 630-mile road race where the only fuel is sunlight
summary: The 30th annual Solar Car Challenge will feature high school teams competing in a 630-mile road race powered entirely by solar energy, with students designing and building their own vehicles from off-the-shelf and 3D-printed components. The five-day competition from Fort Worth to Fort Stockton determines winners by accumulated driven miles.
content:
The 2018 Solar Car Challenge was the last time participants took their designs out on the open road. | Image: Lehman Marks / Solar Car Challenge	

On July 19th, dozens of teams of high school students will begin a five-day, 630-mile road race from Fort Worth to Fort Stockton in Texas. But this is not your typical contest. The students design and build the cars themselves, using off-the-shelf parts and 3D printed materials. The winner is the team that accumulates the most driven miles. And the only fuel they can use to power their Frankenstein-looking vehicles comes from that mass of incandescent gas that hangs in our sky: the Sun.
This year is the 30th anniversary of the annual Solar Car Challenge, a contest that brings together high school students from around the country with the on …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 艰苦的630英里公路赛，唯一的燃料是阳光
zh.summary: 第30届年度太阳能汽车挑战赛将高中队伍汇聚在一场630英里的公路赛中，车辆完全由太阳能驱动，学生使用现成零件和3D打印部件设计和制造自己的车辆。这项从沃思堡到斯托克顿的五天比赛根据累计行驶里程确定获胜者。
